senses.definition: a type of light jacket either worn with pants that do not match or as a uniform by the members of a union, school, club, etc.

examples.x: He wore a navy blazer to the job interview.
A blazer adds a polished look to any outfit.
She paired her blazer with a white blouse and trousers.
The store offers blazers in a variety of colors and styles.
A blazer is perfect for a business casual dress code.

senses.definition: a sleeveless dress worn by girls, primarily in the United Kingdom and Ireland, as a school uniform
a small symbol that students wear to show they belong to a specific school

examples.x: Before leaving for school, he clipped on his school badge to his shirt.
The school badge displayed the school's logo and name in bright colors.
Each student received a school badge when they enrolled in the school.
During the graduation ceremony, students proudly wore their school badges on their gowns.
The teacher reminded the students to wear their school badges on their uniforms every day.

senses.definition: a set of clothing worn for physical education classes or sports activities

examples.x: Students were required to wear their PE kit consisting of shorts, a t-shirt, and sneakers for the gym class.
She packed her PE kit in her backpack for the after-school soccer practice.
The school provided guidelines for the appropriate attire to include in the PE kit, ensuring comfort and safety during physical activities.
His PE kit was neatly folded in his locker, ready for the afternoon basketball game.
Parents were reminded to label their child's PE kit with their name to prevent mix-ups during class.

senses.definition: a loose cloak worn on official occasions or special ceremonies, such as the one worn by a lawyer at court
a black cap with a stiff square top and a tassel, worn as a sign of academic achievement
the traditional attire worn by faculty, staff, and students during academic ceremonies such as graduations, typically including robes, caps, and hoods

examples.x: The professor donned his academic regalia, complete with a doctoral hood, for the commencement ceremony.
Graduates lined up outside the auditorium, wearing their academic regalia in anticipation of the diploma presentation.
The university bookstore offers rentals and purchases of academic regalia for graduating students.
Faculty members proudly wore their academic regalia while leading the procession at the convocation ceremony.
Her academic regalia bore the colors and symbols representing her alma mater and academic achievements.

senses.definition: a hanging ornament made of threads or cords, often found at the end of graduation caps or attached to curtains or clothing

examples.x: As the graduates walked across the stage, the tassels on their caps swayed with each step.
She carefully tied the tassel to the curtain, adding a touch of elegance to the room.
The dancer's costume shimmered with golden tassels as she twirled across the stage.
He absentmindedly played with the tassel on his keychain while waiting for the elevator.
The tailor added delicate tassels to the hem of the dress, giving it a bohemian flair.

senses.definition: a decorative sash worn over the shoulders by graduating students as a symbol of academic achievement or membership in an organization

examples.x: She proudly wore her academic stole adorned with the school's colors and emblem during the commencement ceremony.
The graduating class voted on the design of the academic stole, incorporating meaningful symbols and motifs.
The university bookstore offers a variety of academic stole options for students to choose from, representing different academic honors and achievements.
The honor society members stood out in the crowd with their distinctive gold academic stoles.
Her parents presented her with a special academic stole as a surprise gift to commemorate her accomplishments.

senses.definition: a decorative cord worn around the neck or over the graduation gown by graduating students to signify academic achievement or membership in an honor society

examples.x: She proudly wore the honor cord around her neck, signifying her outstanding academic performance throughout her college years.
Graduating seniors were excited to receive their honor cords during the honors convocation ceremony.
The honor cord, adorned with gold and silver threads, added a touch of distinction to his graduation attire.
Members of the National Honor Society were recognized with a special blue and gold honor cord during the commencement ceremony.
The honor cord served as a visual reminder of the dedication and hard work required to achieve academic excellence.

senses.definition: a garment worn over the shoulders and back, typically with a colorful lining and trim, to signify the academic degree and field of study of the wearer

examples.x: The doctoral candidate proudly wore a hood with gold trim, indicating the completion of their Ph.D. in Psychology.
Each faculty member's hood was unique, reflecting their academic discipline and alma mater.
During the graduation ceremony, students received their hoods as a symbol of their academic achievements.
The blue and white hood represented the bachelor's degree in Education conferred upon the graduate.
The master's degree candidate chose a hood lined with green and gold to represent their field of study in Environmental Science.

senses.definition: a traditional academic cap with a soft crown and a tassel worn by graduates and faculty members during academic ceremonies

examples.x: The professor wore a Tudor bonnet adorned with a black tassel during the graduation ceremony.
As part of the academic regalia, the Tudor bonnet symbolized the achievements of the graduating class.
The dean led the procession wearing a Tudor bonnet, setting the tone for the commencement ceremony.
